insights:
The single best deal is the Qwen3.5 0.8B model, which achieves a value score of 1100.0 by delivering an intelligence rating of 11.0 out of 60 at a blended price of just $0.0100 per million tokens. This model also supports a 262K context window, making it exceptionally cost-effective for simple, high-volume tasks where minimal reasoning is required. Its price-to-intelligence ratio is unmatched, offering the lowest cost per unit of capability among all evaluated models.

Notable runners-up include the DeepSeek V4 Flash (Max), which provides a far higher intelligence of 47.0/60 for $0.0600 per million tokens, yielding a value score of 783.3. It also offers a massive 1000K context window and a speed of 113 tokens per second, making it ideal for complex, long-context tasks at a still-low price. The MiMo-V2-Flash (Feb 2026) is another strong contender, with intelligence of 41.0/60 at the same $0.0600 price, a value score of 683.3, and faster speed at 133 tokens per second, but its context is limited to 256K. The Qwen3.5 4B variant (intelligence 27.0/60, $0.0400/1M) offers a balanced trade-off with a value score of 675.0 and high speed of 169 tokens per second, suitable for mid-range tasks.

For the most common use case—cost-sensitive API use in production—choose the Qwen3.5 0.8B for any task that does not require complex reasoning, such as simple classification, extraction, or high-throughput logging. Its $0.01 per million tokens and 262K context make it the cheapest option by far, allowing you to process massive volumes without budget strain. Reserve the DeepSeek V4 Flash (Max) for the minority of tasks that demand deeper understanding or very long documents, accepting its sixfold higher cost for a fourfold intelligence gain.
